method: pdftotext -layout for canonical PDF text; pandoc -t plain for DOCX text; LibreOffice/PDF rendering for DOCX visual QA; key thesis and factory-resilience pages rendered with Poppler and visually inspected; workbook formula lineage imported from the frozen 2026-06-25 audit, independently rerun on 2026-07-13, and current workbook identities verified by SHA-256

extraction_reproducibility.reason_outputs_not_committed: Full copyrighted source text is reconstructed locally from hash-verified source files and is not redistributed.
extraction_reproducibility.clean_clone_condition: A clean clone plus source files matching the recorded SHA-256 identities and the pinned extractor versions deterministically reproduces every canonical output; --source ID=/path may relocate a source without relaxing its hash check.

sources.authority: primary MFSC physical and ReT source

chapter_map.title: Introduction
chapter_map.extracted_scope: problem, objectives, scope, contributions and thesis structure
chapter_map.claim_boundary: research setting only; no sequential adaptive contract

chapter_map.title: Systematic Literature Review
chapter_map.extracted_scope: SCRES measurement approaches, review protocol, gaps and method classification through 2017
chapter_map.claim_boundary: historical thesis context, not the current Paper 2 literature review

chapter_map.title: Hypotheses Development and Conceptual Framework
chapter_map.extracted_scope: fixed-scenario risk-frequency, inventory-buffer and short-term-capacity hypotheses

chapter_map.title: Research Methods
chapter_map.extracted_scope: simulation, questionnaire/case-study component and non-parametric analysis
chapter_map.claim_boundary: method for the original study; new extensions require fresh verification and validation

chapter_map.title: Operationalization of Resilience in Military Supply Chains
chapter_map.extracted_scope: military-SC semantics; AP/RP/DP algorithms; Equations 5.1-5.5 and accumulated fill-rate branch
chapter_map.claim_boundary: conceptual thesis ReT authority; raw-workbook formula and visible-row population are reconciled separately

chapter_map.title: Simulation Model Output Data and Questionnaire
chapter_map.extracted_scope: Op1-Op13, capacities, demand, risks, assumptions, verification/validation, 90 configurations, run length, warm-up, output matrix and questionnaire
chapter_map.claim_boundary: primary MFSC-physics authority subject to disclosed simplifications

chapter_map.title: Discussion of Results
chapter_map.extracted_scope: risk-frequency effects and moderation by inventory buffers or shifts
chapter_map.claim_boundary: fixed-configuration thesis results, not feedback or learned-policy evidence

chapter_map.title: Conclusions and Future Research
chapter_map.extracted_scope: findings, limitations, practical implications, stationarity, costs, lead time and future questions
chapter_map.claim_boundary: questions and limitations do not supply new-mechanism parameter values

chapter_map.title: Annexes A-E
chapter_map.extracted_scope: text-mining algorithm, Simulink code, questionnaire, ReT categorization and association-rule algorithms
chapter_map.claim_boundary: provenance and implementation evidence, not proof of Python/Simulink distributional identity

search_index.finding: Real MFSC assembles 21 types; the DES uses Cold Weather #1 as a homogeneous simplification.
Twelve raw-material components are specified for the one modeled product.
Assembly rate 320.5 rations/hour; one-shift effective weekly hours/capacity and S1-S3 alternatives are reported.

Processing, batch and transport cadences for the complete network; Op1 PT is 672 h, Op3 releases 15,500 units of each raw material weekly, and the downstream promised order lead time is 48 h after warm-up.
R11-R14, R21-R24 and R3 event assumptions and affected operations.

search_index.topic: order-level ReT equations and conditional aggregation
search_index.finding: Equations 5.1-5.5 define the AP, RP, DP-minus-RP and accumulated fill-rate cases. The repository separately reconstructs the raw-workbook conditional formula and visible-row population contract.
The chain is assemble-to-stock through Op9 and assemble-to-order from Op9 to Op13, with (ROP,Q) logic.
Promised downstream lead time is 48 h after the first 5,000-ration Op9 arrival; deterministic warm-up is 838.8 h; principal runs extend to 20 years or 161,280 h.
Ninety fixed configurations cover risk frequency, inventory buffers and short-term capacity; matched configuration families reuse seeds for comparison.
Original Simulink verification/validation and the case-study questionnaire support the thesis model only; they do not validate later researcher extensions.
The thesis analyzes risk effects and moderation by inventory buffers and shifts across fixed configurations, not sequential feedback policies.
Late orders backorder; cap 60; SPT and contingent priority; overflow is lost.
Vehicle availability and route planning are taken for granted; storage is unlimited.
Setup omitted because one item is modeled; maintenance fixed at 24 hours every seven days.
Steady parameters/demand are a major limitation.
Long-term demand changes should be incorporated quickly through advance cross-functional forecasting.
Lead time is named as a future research dimension.
Land/sea/water/air and urgency/equipment-based mode choice are mentioned without numerical mode physics.
The modeled combat ration is non-perishable at the scale relevant to the thesis; a two-week shelf life is incompatible.

sources.authority: conceptual learning motivation, not an empirical adaptive-policy result

search_index.finding: Explicitly preliminary, theoretical and exploratory.
Twelve-step archetype covers material/information flows, risk identification, assumptions, verification, validation, experimental design, warm-up/run length and metric construction.
Repeated conventional DES runs do not themselves retain experience; the paper calls this an Alzheimer effect and frames the model as open loop.
Proposes inserting a neural/learning mechanism between decision inputs and SCRES outcomes across iterations.
Uses AP, RP, DP and fill rate as example drivers, combined through weights and an activation function; other drivers are explicitly possible.
Proposes progressive adaptive capacity derived from continuous learning from past experiences; this is conceptual, not empirically tested.
Names backpropagation, Kolmogorov-Arnold networks and reinforcement-learning/simulation optimization as possibilities, without a validated action/observation contract or comparative result.

sources.authority: primary source for the published factory-level Cobb-Douglas APP construct, not for the thesis order-level MFSC endpoint

search_index.finding: Hypothetical make-to-stock factory, 36-week aggregate-production-planning horizon, with demand variability as the sole source of uncertainty.
Compares seven fixed pure APP substrategies: three zero-inventory/chase, two constant-workforce and two hybrid rules.
Factory resilience combines average inventory, backorders, spare capacity, net-requirement fulfillment time and normalized total cost.
Log score is 0.024 ln(zeta) - 0.026 ln(epsilon) + 0.04 ln(phi) - 0.06 ln(tau) - 0.1771 ln(kappa_dot); Equation 6 applies a sigmoid.
Coefficients equate each term to 0.2 at its maximum observed after 10,000 factory simulations; they are calibration-specific rather than universal MFSC weights.
Fixed match-chase S12 dominates the other pure APP substrategies, a dominant-constant warning rather than evidence of adaptive state feedback.
The paper validates neither MFSC component mappings nor sequential adaptive, learned or retained-learning value.
Placeholder abstract/results/figures and drafting comments remain.
Describes a SimPy/Gymnasium reconstruction of the 13-operation DES with hourly internal dynamics and 168 h decision cadence.
States a 15-dimensional baseline observation; this prose is a draft and is not a no-privileged-information audit.
States a five-dimensional Track A action over Op3/Op9 dispatch/reorder quantities and shifts, plus later downstream-dispatch extensions.
Uses SC^0.60 * BC^0.25 * AE^0.15 as a sequential Cobb-Douglas-style training reward; it is not canonical ret_excel_request_snapshot_v2.
Contains PPO/RecurrentPPO, HSN and DKANA/KAN prose with unresolved citations and incomplete architecture text.
Includes validation and results prose whose provenance must be reconciled with repository outputs before use.
Any positive prose is superseded by live verdict JSON and corrective audits.

tracked_legacy_extracts.discrepancy_resolution: This tracked file is the default-flow pdftotext rendering of the same hash-verified thesis PDF. The canonical searchable research extraction uses -layout and therefore has SHA-256 5c6101b90a67eae12b52859ac48592ec8c4f2efbdd937f1a7854ce540a75d05c. Both modes reproduce byte-for-byte; the different hashes do not indicate different source documents.

workbook_formula_audit.claim_boundary: establishes formula fidelity, sparse visible-row population lineage and forensic numerical replication; does not establish endogenous DES trajectory or distributional equivalence

copyright_note: The committed artifact is an index and paraphrased extraction note; full source text remains in temporary local extraction files and is not redistributed.
